tldr.title: It's Not Just X. It's Y
tldr.author: Yogthos
bullets: Transition from single LLMs to compound AI systems integrating vector databases like Milvus, orchestration frameworks like LangChain, and evaluation guardrails.
Deploy smaller open-weights models like Llama-3-8B optimized via vLLM rather than relying solely on costly proprietary APIs like GPT-4.
Implement hybrid RAG pipelines using BM25 and dense retrievers instead of relying only on long-context window extensions for information retrieval.

tldr.title: Why does ASTC use ISE when almost nothing else does?
tldr.author: calvin
bullets: ASTC uses Integer Sequence Encoding (ISE) to pack values using non-power-of-two bases like trits (base-3) and quints (base-5).
ISE maximizes coding efficiency by allowing fractional bits per value, enabling fine-grained quantization tradeoffs within ASTC's fixed 128-bit blocks.
Other formats avoid ISE because decoding non-power-of-two bases requires complex integer multipliers and adders, increasing hardware silicon area.

tldr.title: securix: NixOS-based secure operating system. Provides a hardened environment with strong isolation, reproducibility, and policy-driven configurations
tldr.author: koala
bullets: Utilizes Nix's declarative, read-only store to prevent unauthorized runtime system modifications and guarantee reproducible security baselines.
Enforces kernel hardening and strict sandboxing policies to isolate workloads, limiting the blast radius of potential compromises.
Simplifies compliance auditing through cryptographically traceable dependency graphs and reproducible system configurations generated by the Nix build engine.
